rationale: Probes via rdm story mutation-probe on rdm/story_audit/design_gate.py. Covered clauses (KILLED): (1) probes stored as structured data: record_verdict `"probes": probes or [],` -> `"probes": [],` KILLED by the JSON round-trip equality; (2) replay re-executes the recorded killing probes: replay_probes `killed += 1` -> `pass` KILLED by (replayed, killed, failures) == (1, 1, []); (3) replay fails when a probe no longer kills: discarding the SURVIVES failure (`failures.append(f"{di_id}: recorded killing probe now SURVIVES "` -> assign to `_`) KILLED by len(failures) == 1 and 'SURVIVES' in failures[0] after the test is weakened; (4a) non-faithful inputs are shown under --stale: `if stale_only and agg.status == faithfulness.FAITHFUL:` -> `if stale_only:` (skip everything) KILLED by 'DI-1: unreviewed' in the output. UNCOVERED (4b): the filtering-out of faithful inputs - a report that ignores --stale entirely passes (probe recorded as SURVIVED). Fix: add a second, faithful design input to the fixture and assert it absent from the --stale output, then re-review.

probes.find: failures.append(f"{di_id}: recorded killing probe now SURVIVES "

uncovered_clauses: the --stale filter's exclusion of FAITHFUL inputs from the report: the no-filter mutant (`if stale_only and agg.status == faithfulness.FAITHFUL: continue` -> `if False:`) SURVIVES, because the fixture's only design input is non-faithful at the filtered call, so nothing can be asserted absent from the output
